text.title: ADR for Client ARB Review Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ADR for Client ARB Review` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Frontmatter MUST list `arb_approver` (named person + role) + `arb_review_date` (target). Blank = not ready to submit.
rule.rationale: Submitting an unaddressed ADR wastes ARB cycles.

rule.statement: Frontmatter MUST include `regime_constraints` block (regulation citations, internal client policies) that the decision is bound by.
rule.rationale: Constraints surfaced at the top let reviewers reject in seconds, not hours.

rule.statement: ADR MUST declare `redaction_policy: full|partial|none` + which fields are sensitive (contract terms, salary, IP, customer names).
rule.rationale: Without an explicit redaction policy, the ADR cannot be shared post-approval.

rule.statement: Every ADR MUST list ≥ 2 rejected alternatives with one-line rationale each.
Approved ADRs carry a re-review trigger (date or event). 'Final' is forbidden.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
